description: Fix inaccuracies found during static audit of documentation against source code. Covers stale CLI flags, missing MCP client configs, wrong API tables, incorrect scaffolding claims, wrong dependency graphs, and incorrect health endpoint description.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add missing --force flag and browser-tool. listing to CLI reference
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the CLI reference to document all available flags so that I can use them correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: The `opentabs config set` section documents the `-f, --force` flag with description: allows `localPlugins.add` to register paths that do not yet exist on disk

The `opentabs config set` section documents that `opentabs config set browser-tool.` (with trailing dot and no value) lists all available browser tool names from the running server, parallel to the existing `tool.` documentation
lastUpdated frontmatter is updated to 2026-03-01
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx. The `opentabs config set` section starts around line 616. Source of truth for the --force flag: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts line 751 — `.option('-f, --force', 'Force localPlugins.add even if the path does not exist yet')`. Source for browser-tool. listing: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts lines 596-599 — `if (key === BROWSER_TOOL_PREFIX) { return handleListBrowserTools(options); }`. The existing docs already document `tool.` trailing-dot behavior around line 657 — add `browser-tool.` in the same pattern. For the --force flag, add it to the flags table or description in the config set section.

userStories.title: Add Windsurf config and fix Cursor config in MCP server reference
userStories.description: As a user setting up an MCP client, I want accurate configuration examples so that my client connects successfully.
acceptanceCriteria: The Cursor configuration example includes the `"type": "http"` field in the JSON, matching what `opentabs start --show-config` actually generates
A new Windsurf configuration section is added with the correct JSON format using `serverUrl` (not `url`) and file path `~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json`, matching the source code
lastUpdated frontmatter is updated to 2026-03-01
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx. The MCP client configuration examples are around lines 560-620. Source of truth: platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts lines 184-200. Cursor config (lines 189-190) uses `{ type: 'http', url: mcpUrl }` — add `"type": "http"` to the existing Cursor JSON example. Windsurf config (lines 195-200) uses `{ serverUrl: mcpUrl }` with file path `~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json` — add a new subsection after Cursor following the same format as the other client examples. The Windsurf JSON structure is: `{ "mcpServers": { "opentabs": { "serverUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:9515/mcp", "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer <secret>" } } } }`.

userStories.title: Fix unauthenticated /health response description in troubleshooting
userStories.description: As a user debugging connection issues, I want the troubleshooting guide to accurately describe what the health endpoint returns so that I can interpret responses correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: The troubleshooting page correctly states that unauthenticated `/health` returns only `{ "status": "ok" }` — NOT `status`, `version`, and `extensionConnected`
lastUpdated frontmatter is updated to 2026-03-01
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx, around line 224. The current text says: 'Without a Bearer token, returns only status, version, and extensionConnected.' This is wrong. Source of truth: platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts lines 265-268 — `if (!authenticated) { return Response.json({ status: 'ok' }); }`. The unauthenticated response is just `{ "status": "ok" }` with no other fields. The MCP server reference (docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx lines 130-133) already documents this correctly — make troubleshooting consistent with it.

userStories.title: Add missing 'other 4xx' row to httpStatusToToolError table in SDK utilities
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the status code mapping table to be complete so that I know what ToolError is returned for any HTTP status.
acceptanceCriteria: The `httpStatusToToolError` status code mapping table includes a row for 4xx status codes not individually listed (e.g., 405, 406, 409, 410, etc.) showing: no category (undefined), retryable: false
The row is placed between the 429 row and the 503 row (or between the 4xx and 5xx groups)
lastUpdated frontmatter is updated to 2026-03-01
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx, the httpStatusToToolError table around lines 441-452. Source of truth: platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts lines 20-45. The source has three fallback branches after specific status checks: (1) `status >= 500` at line 36 → category: 'internal', retryable: true; (2) `status >= 400 && status < 500` at line 41 → NO category (undefined), retryable: false; (3) final else at line 44 → category: 'internal', retryable: false. The docs table has rows for specific 4xx codes and a 5xx row and an 'Other' row, but is MISSING the 'other 4xx' row (branch 2). Add a row like: '| 4xx (other) | — | `false` | Other client errors |' between the 429 row and 503 row. The 'Other' row at the bottom maps to branch 3 (non-4xx, non-5xx).

userStories.title: Fix scaffolding auto-registration claim in first-plugin and plugin-development guides
userStories.description: As a new plugin developer, I want accurate documentation about when my plugin gets registered so that I follow the correct workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: first-plugin.mdx no longer claims that scaffolding auto-registers the plugin with the MCP server — instead correctly states that registration happens during the first `npm run build` (which runs `opentabs-plugin build`)
plugin-development.mdx line ~71 no longer claims the scaffolder auto-registers — instead states registration happens during the first build
The rest of plugin-development.mdx (which already correctly says registration happens during build around line 475) remains consistent
lastUpdated frontmatter is updated to 2026-03-01 on both files
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Two files to edit:
1. docs/content/docs/first-plugin.mdx — lines 23-24 say 'This creates a standalone npm package and auto-registers it with the MCP server.' Change to something like 'This creates a standalone npm package. The plugin is registered with the MCP server on first build.'
2. docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx — line 71 says 'The scaffolder also auto-registers the plugin in ~/.opentabs/config.json so the MCP server discovers it immediately.' Remove or rewrite to say registration happens during the first `opentabs-plugin build` (triggered by `npm run build`). Note: the same file correctly describes this at line ~475 ('Auto-registers the plugin in ~/.opentabs/config.json (first build only)') — make line 71 consistent with that.
Source of truth: platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts — the scaffoldPlugin function (lines 472-537) only creates files, does NOT write to config.json. Registration is in platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts line 168.

userStories.title: Fix dependency graph in publishing guide
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the publishing guide to show the correct dependency graph so that I publish packages in the right order.
acceptanceCriteria: mcp-server is shown as depending only on shared (NOT on browser-extension)
create-plugin is shown as depending only on cli (NOT on plugin-sdk and shared)
The overall publish order remains coherent with the corrected dependency graph
lastUpdated frontmatter is updated to 2026-03-01
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx.
1. Around line 34-35: mcp-server is listed as depending on `shared, browser-extension`. Actual: only depends on `@opentabs-dev/shared`. Verified in platform/mcp-server/package.json line 27.
2. Around lines 53-55: create-plugin is listed as depending on `plugin-sdk, shared`. Actual: only depends on `@opentabs-dev/cli`. Verified in platform/create-plugin/package.json line 21.
Update both the dependency list/table and any publish order descriptions to reflect the correct graph. The corrected publish order should still work bottom-up from shared → other packages → cli → create-plugin.
